channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: The best early deals you can already grab from Amazon’s Big Spring Sale
item.description: Amazon’s Prime Day sale may arrive earlier than usual this year, but there’s a deal event happening much sooner: Amazon’s annual Big Spring Sale, which is happening March 25th through the 31st....

item.title: Day 48 of #100DayOfCode — Deployment I: Deploy Backend
item.description: Earlier, on Day 40, I built the backend for my auth system. Then on Day 44, I improved it by adding TypeScript and Zod for type safety and validation. Today (Day 48), the goal was simple: 👉 Deploy...

item.title: Guess the Number Higher or Lower
item.description: Introduction This problem is based on a guessing game where we need to find a hidden number efficiently. Instead of checking every number one by one, we can use Binary Search, which drastically...

item.title: Valid Anagram
item.description: Valid Anagram Problem Given two strings s and t, check if t is an anagram of s. An anagram means both strings contain the same characters with the same frequency, just arranged differently....

item.title: Git -> Quick Drill Sheet
item.description: Important concepts you understood: Working Directory 👉 Where you edit files Staging Area 👉 Temporary place before commit Commit 👉 Snapshot of project Local Repository 👉 Git history on your machine...

item.title: "I Built a Quantum Physics Simulator in JavaScript — And It Broke My Brain"
item.description: I Built a Quantum Physics Simulator in JavaScript — And It Broke My Brain I'm going to tell you something strange. An electron can pass through two holes at the same time. Not metaphorically. Not...

item.title: Guess the Number Higher or Lower [Leetcode]
item.description: Guess Number Higher or Lower Problem You’re playing a game where a number is picked between 1 and n. You have to guess it using an API that tells you: -1 → your guess is too high 1 → your guess is...

item.title: KickJS Module Generator: 4 Architecture Patterns for Every Backend Need
item.description: One of the things I appreciate most about working with a framework is when it meets me where I am. Not every feature I build needs the same level of ceremony. A health check endpoint does not need...

item.title: KickJS Query Engine Deep Dive: Filtering, Sorting, Search, and Pagination with MongoDB
item.description: KickJS Query Engine Deep Dive: Filtering, Sorting, Search, and Pagination with MongoDB When you're building an API with 60+ endpoints and 10+ list views, you can't afford inconsistency. Some...

item.title: Seeing the Positive In the AI Race
item.description: The AI Race Is Scary and Uncertain I won't deny that the AI race has its negative effect. But I want to explore some of the positives that might come out of it and why I think it's good to see...

item.title: OpenMusic - My first Open source project
item.description: OpenMusic (formerly SocyMusic) OpenMusic began as my venture into creating the ultimate free open-source music player for Android devices. My aim was simple: a clean, minimalist design packed with...

item.title: Elon Musk unveils chip manufacturing plans for SpaceX and Tesla
item.description: Elon Musk recently outlined ambitious plans for a chip-building collaboration Tesla and SpaceX — but he has a history of overpromising.

item.title: Delve accused of misleading customers with ‘fake compliance’
item.description: An anonymous Substack post accuses compliance startup Delve of “falsely” convincing “hundreds of customers they were compliant” with privacy and security regulations.

item.title: Musk says he’s building Terafab chip plant in Austin, Texas
item.description: Elon Musk announced plans to build a Terafab plant in Austin, Texas, that will be jointly run by Tesla and SpaceX. The goal is to eventually build chips at scale for robotics, artificial...

item.title: The secret story of the vocoder, the military tech that changed music forever
item.description: The vocoder was never supposed to be a revolution in music. It wasn't supposed to be anything in music, really. Its development began a century ago, when an engineer at Bell Labs was looking for a...

item.title: The pint-sized Sonos Roam 2 is more than 20 percent off this weekend
item.description: Sonos is no stranger to the occasional sale. The iconic audio company routinely runs sales around Black Friday, Amazon Prime Day, and the Super Bowl, though it’s rare to see substantial discounts...

item.title: An exclusive tour of Amazon’s Trainium lab, the chip that’s won over Anthropic, OpenAI, even Apple
item.description: Shortly after Amazon announced its $50 billion investment in OpenAI, AWS invited me on a private tour of the chip lab at the heart of the deal.

item.title: AI was everywhere at gaming’s big developer conference — except the games
item.description: AI was everywhere at the GDC Festival of Gaming this year. Vendors at the event pitched generative AI tools for things like making AI-driven NPCs and even entire games from a chat box. On the show...

item.title: Online age checks came first — a VPN crackdown could be next
item.description: This is The Stepback, a weekly newsletter breaking down one essential story from the tech world. For more news about online age verification and your privacy, follow Emma Roth. The Stepback...

item.title: Are AI tokens the new signing bonus or just a cost of doing business?
item.description: Maybe tokens really will become the fourth pillar of engineering compensation. But engineers might want to hold the line before embracing this as a straightforward win.

item.title: Halide co-founder is suing former partner for bringing source code to Apple
item.description: Lux Optics co-founder Sebastiaan de With made headlines when he joined Apple in late January. The company was behind Halide, one of the most popular photography apps for the iPhone, which gained a...

item.title: Publisher pulls horror novel ‘Shy Girl’ over AI concerns
item.description: Hachette Book Group said it will not be publishing “Shy Girl” over concerns that artificial intelligence was used to generate the text.

item.title: The AirPods Pro 3 are $50 off right now, nearly matching their best-ever price
item.description: Less than a week ago, Apple announced the forthcoming AirPods Max 2, a pair of over-ear headphones that leverage the company’s H2 chip for AI-powered live translation, conversation awareness, and...

item.title: Why Wall Street wasn’t won over by Nvidia’s big conference
item.description: Despite investor fears of an AI bubble, Nvidia's latest conference shows that most in the industry aren't concerned by that possibility.

item.title: Here are 20 of our favorite outdoor deals from REI’s Member Days Sale
item.description: REI’s latest sale is in full swing. The outdoor retailer’s exclusive shopping event runs through Monday, March 23rd, bringing with it a batch of discounts on Garmin watches, tents, portable...

item.title: The gen AI Kool-Aid tastes like eugenics
item.description: Like many people, director Valerie Veatch was intrigued when OpenAI first released its Sora text-to-video generative AI model to the public in 2024. Though she didn't fully understand the...

item.title: FBI Warns Russian Hackers Target Signal, WhatsApp in Mass Phishing Attacks
item.description: Threat actors affiliated with Russian Intelligence Services are conducting phishing campaigns to compromise commercial messaging applications (CMAs) like WhatsApp and Signal to seize control of...

item.title: I Tried DoorDash’s Tasks App and Saw the Bleak Future of AI Gig Work
item.description: I recorded videos of myself doing laundry, scrambling eggs, and walking around the park in DoorDash’s new Tasks app, where gig workers are paid to train AI.

item.title: Cyberattack on a Car Breathalyzer Firm Leaves Drivers Stuck
item.description: Plus: The FBI admits it’s buying phone data to track Americans, Iranian hackers disrupt medical care at Maryland hospitals, and more.

item.title: Oracle Patches Critical CVE-2026-21992 Enabling Unauthenticated RCE in Identity Manager
item.description: Oracle has released security updates to address a critical security flaw impacting Identity Manager and Web Services Manager that could be exploited to achieve remote code execution. The...
